locations.name: 卡尔·林奈
locations.summary: 卡尔·冯·林奈（瑞典語：Carl von Linné；1707年5月23日—1778年1月10日），也譯為林内、林耐，原名卡尔·林奈乌斯（Carl Linnaeus），瑞典生物学家，瑞典科学院创始人兼第一任主席。林奈奠定了现代生物学命名法…

locations.name: 開普敦
locations.summary: 開普敦（英語：Cape Town，阿非利卡語：Kaapstad [ˈkɑːpstat]，科薩語：iKapa），亦稱好望角市、地角市或開普城，是南非人口排名第二大城市，也是開普敦市都會市鎮組成部分、西開普省省會。開普敦為南非立法首都，因此國…

locations.name: 马坎达
locations.summary: 馬坎達（Makhanda），原名格雷厄姆斯敦（英語：Grahamstown），是南非東開普省的城鎮，位於該國東南部，距離伊莉莎白港130公里，始建於1820年，面積27.28平方公里，2001年人口21,403，人口密度每平方公里780人…

relations.subject: Southern black korhaan
relations.evidence: The southern black korhaan (Afrotis afra), also known as the black bustard, is a species of bird in the bustard family, Otididae. This small bustard is found in southwestern South Africa, from Namaqualand, south to Cape Town and east to Makhanda. It prefers semi-arid habitats such as grasslands, shrublands and savannas where it can easily prey on ground-dwelling arthropods and eat seeds.{{Cite journal|last1=Kok|first1=O

relations.subject: Southern black korhaan
relations.evidence: As of 2020, its estimated suitable habitat throughout its range is approximately . It prefers open, semi-arid habitats such as shrub-lands and savannahs where it can easily prey on grass-dwelling arthropods, also grasslands adjoining marshland.{{Cite journal|last=Winterbottom|first=J
relations.object_summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

relations.subject: Southern black korhaan
relations.evidence: A.|date=1990-12-01|title=Diet of the Black Korhaan Eupodotis Afra in the Orange Free State and North-West Cape|journal=Ostrich|volume=61|issue=3–4|pages=107–110|doi=10.1080/00306525.1990.9634155|issn=0030-6525}} It reproduces yearly in the spring and will lay about one or two eggs per breeding season. Numbers have declined rapidly as much of its habitat has been converted to agricultural land and remaining tracts are often fragmented. Due to this habitat destruction, the species is considered vulnerable by the IUCN Red List
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

relations.subject: Southern black korhaan
relations.evidence: Recent studies have shown that the southern black korhaan's population has been decreasing lately, which has raised concerns about its conservation status. The species was once very common but it is now becoming rarer as its habitat is being fragmented. Hence, it has been deemed vulnerable by the IUCN Red List of threatened species since October 1, 2016. The primary threat to these species is the conversion of natural vegetation to agricultural lands as well as aquaculture. This has caused habitat fragmentation and a reduction in available food sources. This reduction in habitat also means less suitable breeding grounds, which has not only affected breeding success, but chick and egg survival rates. Indeed, agricultural lands do not provide sufficient plant cover to protect them from predators such as the pied crow. Other threats include climate change, human disturbance and diseases
relations.object_summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。
